questions.q: What is the general formula for primary amines?
questions.explanation: Primary amines have one amino group attached to the alkyl chain, hence the general formula is RNH2.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a secondary amine?
questions.explanation: Secondary amines have two alkyl groups attached to the amino group, so dimethylamine is a correct example.

questions.q: What is the pKa value of aniline?
questions.explanation: Aniline is a weak base, and its pKa value is around 9.4.

questions.q: Which of the following reactions is characteristic of amines?
questions.explanation: Amines can undergo alkylation reactions, where an alkyl group is added to the amino group.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding a strong acid to a solution of a secondary amine?
opts: (B) It will form a tertiary amine
(C) It will form a quaternary ammonium salt
questions.explanation: When a strong acid is added to a secondary amine, it will form a quaternary ammonium salt.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of tertiary amines?
opts: (B) They can form quaternary ammonium salts
(C) They are generally less basic than primary amines
(D) They are generally more basic than primary amines
questions.explanation: Tertiary amines are generally less basic than primary amines due to steric hindrance.

questions.q: What is the name of the reaction where an amine is oxidized to form an aldehyde or ketone?
questions.explanation: Oxidative deamination is a reaction where an amine is oxidized to form an aldehyde or ketone.

questions.q: Which of the following amines can form a quaternary ammonium salt?
questions.explanation: Tertiary amines like triethylamine can form quaternary ammonium salts.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding a strong acid to a solution of a primary amine?
opts: (B) It will form a tertiary amine
(C) It will form a quaternary ammonium salt
questions.explanation: When a strong acid is added to a primary amine, it will form a salt.

questions.q: Which of the following reactions is characteristic of aromatic amines?
questions.explanation: Aromatic amines can undergo aromatic substitution reactions.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding a strong base to a solution of a quaternary ammonium salt?
opts: (A) It will form a tertiary amine
(B) It will form a secondary amine
(C) It will form a primary amine
questions.explanation: When a strong base is added to a quaternary ammonium salt, it will form a primary amine.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of aniline?
What is the pKa value of methylamine?
questions.explanation: Methylamine is a weak base, and its pKa value is around 3.4.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a quaternary ammonium salt?
questions.explanation: Tetramethylammonium chloride is a quaternary ammonium salt.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding a strong acid to a solution of aniline?
opts: (B) It will form a tertiary amine
(C) It will form a quaternary ammonium salt
questions.explanation: When a strong acid is added to aniline, it will form a salt.

questions.q: Which of the following reactions is characteristic of secondary amines?
questions.explanation: Secondary amines can undergo alkylation reactions.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding a strong base to a solution of a tertiary amine?
opts: (B) It will form a secondary amine
(C) It will form a primary amine
questions.explanation: When a strong base is added to a tertiary amine, it will form a primary amine.

questions.q: Which of the following amines is a base?
What is the effect of adding a strong acid to a solution of a tertiary amine?
opts: (B) It will form a secondary amine
(C) It will form a quaternary ammonium salt
questions.explanation: When a strong acid is added to a tertiary amine, it will form a quaternary ammonium salt.
